_comment: Forge SELF-DEVELOPMENT prompt-suite state machine (Forge of Forge). This is NOT the adopter seed — it tracks Forge's own improvement roadmap and is excluded from the adopter export (see ../../forge.manifest.json -> selfOnly). next_prompt.py (copied beside it) selects the next eligible prompt by topological dependsOn order. New work items are recorded here by /forge-contribute. Schema reference: ../../prompts/state.schema.md.

prompts.title: Make Forge self-hosting (self/ workspace, manifest + export, forge-selfcheck gate, /forge-contribute, framework CI)
forge-export: adopter copy has no dead self-only targets and is docs-fresh
forge-validate: adopter project integrity gate + optional strict-validation CI selectable at /forge-init
Multi-contributor hardening: sharded claims + merge queue/required checks + union-merge for derived docs + teams guide
Readiness audit: scale + async-collaboration gap review with an evidence-grounded improvement backlog (ADR-S5)
Prompt context discipline: just-in-time @requirement retrieval + load-bearing instructions at start/end + worked examples (context budget)
Module-scoped requirements + scoped/incremental derived-docs regeneration (per-module traceability/changelog/status)
Claim lifecycle hardening: heartbeat/TTL + retry count + auto-release of dead claims + auto-blocked after N failures
WIP limits + dependency-aware scheduling: forge.config WIP cap, claim at most N, prefer prompts that unblock the most dependents
Source-of-truth conflict guard: forge-validate fails on git conflict markers + verifies ref->requirement consistency pre-merge
Optional module/requirement ownership metadata (inverse-Conway routing) surfaced in STATUS and the teams guide
Reviewer scaling: per-module review scoping (parallelizable) + a review->reflect->retry self-correction loop
